tensions.text: The tamBase crossCheck attributes a '10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR (IndexBox)' as the comparator that exposed the €495M ceiling as contaminated, but the only IndexBox figure surfaced with that exact 10-12% range comes from the EU 'Power and Cable Management' report (€8.5–9.5B market), not a rack-PDU-specific report — the same definitional-bundling problem the refinement was trying to escape.

No segmentation table was found isolating a rack-PDU-only revenue/CAGR figure from within BIS/R&M's $2.45B Europe PDUs+PSUs category — the search intent (settling whether €495M measures rack-PDU or the broader category) remains unresolved by direct evidence; the tamBase derivation's removal of the €495M ceiling rests on category-label reasoning, not on a sourced sub-segment split.

IndexBox maintains two separately-titled EU reports — 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' and 'Data Center Power Distribution Units' — implying even IndexBox does not treat 'rack PDU' as a single unambiguous scope, undercutting the promotionPath's assumption that commissioning 'the' IndexBox EU rack-PDU report would cleanly pin the base.

absences: No BIS/R&M or Research and Markets table was found that isolates rack-PDU-only revenue or CAGR as a sub-segment distinct from PSUs within the $2.45B Europe category — the segmentation split needed to settle the cross-check ceiling question was not surfaced.

IndexBox's EU-scoped 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' and 'Data Center Power Distribution Units' report pages disclose no headline market-size figures in the free executive summaries — both remain paywalled with only narrative teasers.
Country-level IndexBox rack-PDU reports for Germany and the UK likewise disclose no headline figures, so no bottom-up country validation of the €300M CE-7 estimate is available from these results.
No source in this batch gives a rack-PDU-specific (as opposed to PDU+PSU or Power-and-Cable-Management-bundled) CAGR figure directly comparable to the cited 10-12% 'intelligent-PDU' rate.
